nodes.name: import-bidar.mjs
nodes.summary: One-time data import script converting the real Techno-Futures from Bidar annotated map (COMPOST Issue 01) into Archie format, fetching annotations and JPEG dimensions via HTTP and converting circle coordinates to xywh bounding rects.

nodes.name: jpegDims
nodes.summary: Parses JPEG binary header to extract image dimensions (width, height) by scanning for the SOF (Start of Frame) marker in the byte stream.

nodes.name: import-voynich.mjs
nodes.summary: Import script that reads Voynich annotation sources, parses selector geometries, and produces the published archive for the multi-reading exhibit demo.

nodes.name: jpegDims
nodes.summary: Parses JPEG dimensions from raw buffer bytes by scanning segment markers for SOF0 width/height.

nodes.name: selectorToRect
nodes.summary: Parses W3C fragment selector (xywh=pixel or xywh=percent) to a bounding rect with pixel-to-percent conversion for the non-100% case.

nodes.name: portable.test.ts
nodes.summary: Test suite for Approach P proving data parity, blob URL media resolution, revoke lifecycle, and zero-sink-change for the portable Viewer seam.

nodes.name: portable.ts
nodes.summary: Approach P implementation that reads exhibit data from an in-memory ZipFilesystem and mints blob: URLs for embedded media assets, rewriting object sources and note-body markdown references.

nodes.name: mintAssetBlob
nodes.summary: Reads an embedded asset file from the zip filesystem and creates a blob: URL with the correct MIME type.

nodes.name: rewriteAssetUrl
nodes.summary: Rewrites an asset-relative URL containing /assets/ to a blob: URL, leaving external URLs unchanged.

nodes.name: rewriteNoteBodyMedia
nodes.summary: Rewrites /assets/ tokens in W3C annotation note bodies to blob: URLs so NoteMedia/NoteLightbox render without component changes.

nodes.name: loadPortableExhibit
nodes.summary: Main exported function that loads a published exhibit from a ZipFilesystem, rewriting all embedded media references to blob: URLs, and returns a PortableLoad with revoke lifecycle.

nodes.name: sw-bootstrap.ts
nodes.summary: Approach S bootstrap providing a MutableRegistry for managing ZipFilesystem instances and browser-only Service Worker registration and bytes-handoff functions.

nodes.name: MutableRegistry
nodes.summary: In-memory registry mapping library IDs to ZipFilesystem instances, used by the Service Worker to serve portable exhibit assets.

nodes.name: bootstrapPage
nodes.summary: Browser-side function that registers the Service Worker, waits for readiness, and hands over zip bytes via postMessage.

nodes.name: installServiceWorker
nodes.summary: Service Worker side function that installs a message listener for archi:open handoffs and registers the ZipFilesystem.

nodes.name: sw-handler.test.ts
nodes.summary: Test suite for Approach S proving handler routing, media serving, 404 handling, and cache isolation between libraries.

nodes.name: sw-handler.ts
nodes.summary: Approach S Service Worker fetch handler that serves published exhibit files from a ZipFilesystem registry, with URL parsing, content-type mapping, and cache isolation via library-namespaced keys.

nodes.name: parsePublishedUrl
nodes.summary: Parses a request URL into (libraryId, relPath) if it matches the published namespace, or returns undefined for fall-through.

nodes.name: cacheKeyFor
nodes.summary: Generates a library-namespaced cache key to prevent cross-library cache reads.

nodes.name: handleArchiveFetch
nodes.summary: Pure SW fetch handler that serves archive file bytes with correct content-type, returns 404 for missing files, or undefined for non-published URLs.

nodes.name: fixture.ts
nodes.summary: Shared spike test fixture that builds an in-memory published .archie.zip with an embedded asset image, using the same publishLibrary path as production code.

nodes.name: buildLog
nodes.summary: Builds an AnnotationLog with a head note and a media note containing an /assets/ markdown reference for testing media URL rewriting.

nodes.name: buildArchiveBytes
nodes.summary: Publishes the fixture library to a ZipFilesystem with embedded asset bytes and serializes to .archie.zip bytes.

nodes.name: openArchive
nodes.summary: Opens a Uint8Array archive as a ZipFilesystem, simulating the portable Viewer open flow.

nodes.name: buildArchiveBytesAlt
nodes.summary: Builds a second, distinct archive with a different exhibit title for testing cache isolation.

nodes.name: site.ts
nodes.summary: Core site publishing engine: serializes a Library + annotation log into a IIIF-based static site on a Zip filesystem, with asset bundling, head rewriting, and annotation page generation.

nodes.name: rewriteHeadBodies
nodes.summary: Rewrites annotation head body content including Archie link resolution for published sites.

nodes.name: publishLibrary
nodes.summary: Serializes a complete library with all exhibits, assets, annotations, and reading-specific head pages into a IIIF-compliant published site on a filesystem.

nodes.name: libraryToZipFs
nodes.summary: Convenience wrapper that publishes a library to an in-memory Zip filesystem.

nodes.name: libraryToZip
nodes.summary: Convenience wrapper that publishes a library and returns the raw Zip buffer.

nodes.name: loadLibrary
nodes.summary: Loads a previously-published library from a filesystem, reconstructing exhibits with objects, annotations, and rights metadata.

nodes.name: readPublishedExhibit
nodes.summary: Reads a single published exhibit from a filesystem including manifest, canvas annotations, and rights.

nodes.name: voynich-readings.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests for the Voynich manuscript reading-specific annotation publishing flow, verifying per-reading annotation pages in the published site.

nodes.name: buildLog
nodes.summary: Test helper building a multi-reading annotation log for Voynich manuscript interop tests.

nodes.name: filter.ts
nodes.summary: Filter utilities for annotation records supporting queries by reading, layer, tag, and base-note status.

nodes.name: tagsOf
nodes.summary: Extracts tag values from annotation record bodies with tagging purpose.

nodes.name: layersOf
nodes.summary: Extracts layer identifiers from annotation record bodies.

nodes.name: readingOf
nodes.summary: Extracts the reading identifier from an annotation record.

nodes.name: filterByReading
nodes.summary: Filters annotation records to those matching a specific reading ID.

nodes.name: baseNotes
nodes.summary: Filters records to only those that are base-level notes (no layer).

nodes.name: allReadings
nodes.summary: Collects all unique reading IDs from a set of annotation records.

nodes.name: filterByLayer
nodes.summary: Filters annotation records to those on a specific layer.

nodes.name: filterByTag
nodes.summary: Filters annotation records to those with a specific tag value.

nodes.name: allLayers
nodes.summary: Collects all unique layer IDs from a set of annotation records.

nodes.name: allTags
nodes.summary: Collects all unique tag values from a set of annotation records.

nodes.name: reading.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests for the reading-based annotation query system, verifying log projection and head filtering across reading boundaries.

nodes.name: note
nodes.summary: Test helper creating annotation records with optional tags for reading query tests.

nodes.name: session.ts
nodes.summary: Annotation session manager providing transactional CRUD over an annotation log with automatic conflict detection and resolution.

nodes.name: AnnotationSession
nodes.summary: Annotation session manager providing transactional CRUD over an annotation log with auto-conflict detection, resolution, and OPFS-based persistence.

nodes.name: deserialize.ts
nodes.summary: Deserialization utilities that convert WADM-formatted history annotation pages back into the internal append-only log format.

nodes.name: recordFromHistoryAnnotation
nodes.summary: Converts a single WADM history annotation into an internal AnnotationRecord with full field mapping.

nodes.name: fromHistoryPage
nodes.summary: Converts a WADM AnnotationPage into an array of internal annotation records.

nodes.name: fromHistory
nodes.summary: Converts an array of WADM history pages into a flat annotation log.

nodes.name: log.ts
nodes.summary: Append-only annotation log implementation (ADR-0003 / Q-3) providing appendNew, appendEdit, appendDelete operations with revision IDs and ISO timestamps.

nodes.name: append
nodes.summary: Appends a record to the annotation log and freezes the resulting array.

nodes.name: linearHead
nodes.summary: Finds the linear head revision for a given logical ID in the annotation log.

nodes.name: appendNew
nodes.summary: Creates and appends a new annotation record with a minted logical ID and revision ID.

nodes.name: appendEdit
nodes.summary: Creates and appends an edit record for an existing annotation, referencing the previous head revision.

nodes.name: appendDelete
nodes.summary: Creates and appends a tombstone record marking an annotation as deleted.

nodes.name: serialize.ts
nodes.summary: WADM-compliant serialization producing heads AnnotationPages (current versions with prov:wasRevisionOf and archie:hasHistory link-outs) and per-logicalId history sidecars with full version chains. Implements citation id disambiguation (Q-6) for concurrent merge collisions by appending ~{rev} suffixes.
nodes.languageNotes: Citation id disambiguation uses lexicographic comparison of ULID revs for stable ordering regardless of merge order — the common no-collision case keeps clean URIs.

nodes.name: dedupe
nodes.summary: Computes dedupe.

nodes.name: citationIds
nodes.summary: Internal function computing collision-disambiguated citation IDs for every record in the log. Ensures valid JSON-LD under concurrent merge collisions by appending ~{rev} suffixes (Q-6).

nodes.name: recordToAnnotation
nodes.summary: Converts a single AnnotationRecord into a base WADM Annotation with explicit id, preserving target, body, motivation, and modified timestamp. Optionally includes the @context for standalone use.

nodes.name: withDagMeta
nodes.summary: Computes withDagMeta.

nodes.name: targetSource
nodes.summary: Extracts the source IRI from an annotation record's target field, supporting both string targets and SpecificResource shapes.

nodes.name: headsPageFromRecords
nodes.summary: Builds a W3C AnnotationPage from pre-selected head records, attaching archie:hasHistory and prov:wasRevisionOf provenance link-outs per annotation for IIIF interoperability.

nodes.name: headsPagesByReading
nodes.summary: Partitions a canvas's head records into one AnnotationPage per Reading plus a base page for unassigned notes. Implements ADR-0007's multi-AnnotationPage Canvas pattern for IIIF viewer toggling, with stable base-first ordering.

nodes.name: toHeadsPage
nodes.summary: Pure function producing the canvas heads AnnotationPage from the full log: only current head versions per logicalId, each with citation ids and provenance links.

nodes.name: citationIdMap
nodes.summary: Builds a logicalId-to-citation-id map for an annotation log with collision-disambiguated ids.

nodes.name: toHistory
nodes.summary: Pure function building per-logicalId history sidecar AnnotationPages with the full version chain, plus an index mapping logicalId to page URL.

nodes.name: breadcrumb.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests for the breadcrumb navigation model: gallery/exhibit/section-level trails with label resolution and hash targets.

nodes.name: breadcrumb.ts
nodes.summary: Builds a clickable breadcrumb trail for exhibit navigation (Gallery > Exhibit > Note), translating ViewerRoutes into Crumb objects with navigable hashes. Pure model logic testable without the viewer shell.

nodes.name: breadcrumbFor
nodes.summary: Builds an ordered breadcrumb trail from a ViewerRoute and resolved display labels. Always includes the Gallery at top level, adds exhibit and optionally note levels as appropriate.

nodes.name: route.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests for hash-based viewer routing (parseRoute/routeToHash round-trip): gallery, exhibit, deep-link, and ?src= hosted-zip pointer across various edge cases.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Hash-based router for the viewer app, parsing location hashes into structured ViewerRoute objects (gallery/exhibit with optional note deep-link) and serializing routes back to canonical hashes. Supports ?src= hosted-zip composition per ADR-0009.

nodes.name: parseRoute
nodes.summary: Parses a location hash into a structured ViewerRoute (gallery/exhibit with optional note deep-link and ?src= composition). Never throws on garbage input — falls back to gallery view.

nodes.name: routeToHash
nodes.summary: Inverse of parseRoute, building a canonical hash string from a ViewerRoute for link generation, breadcrumb targets, and browser navigation.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: W3C Web Annotation Data Model (WADM) type definitions and Archie spine metadata constants: full annotation model with bodies, targets, selectors, and DAG extensions. Locally defined to keep @render/core renderer-free.
nodes.languageNotes: WADM types defined locally to keep @render/core pure and testable without the Annotorious renderer dependency.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration for the render-core package, extending the project base config with rootDir set to src and outDir set to dist for library output.

nodes.name: serialize.test.ts
nodes.summary: Test suite for the WADM serialization layer, validating heads-page and history-sidecar output including citation id assignment, prov:wasRevisionOf linking, plural-head collision disambiguation (Q-6), and JSON round-trip shape preservation for rect and polygon selectors.

nodes.name: log.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests for the append-only log operations: appendNew, appendEdit, and appendDelete operations.

nodes.name: vitest.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest test runner configuration for the @render/mount package, enabling headless unit testing of the mount layer logic.

nodes.name: published.ts

nodes.summary: Published library factory building three Voynich exhibits (Rosettes single, Whole Manuscript grid, Reading the Unreadable narrative) from shared seed data, each with its own annotation log routed to the correct slug.

nodes.name: Credit.svelte
nodes.summary: Rights display component showing a quiet credit line with an expandable about-and-rights panel for IIIF requiredStatement and license information, used across gallery, exhibit, and reader views.

nodes.name: EmptyHall.svelte
nodes.summary: Portable-mode landing page shown when no library is loaded, presenting a dashed invitation frame with file-pick and drag-drop support for opening .archie.zip files in curator voice.

nodes.name: ExhibitView.svelte
nodes.summary: Exhibit layout orchestrator that loads published exhibit data, resolves the layout type (single/grid/narrative) via resolveLayout, and renders the appropriate component with annotation and reading state management.

nodes.name: Gallery.svelte
nodes.summary: Library gallery component rendering exhibit cards from exhibits.json on the warm gallery wall, each card linking via the hash router to its exhibit view.

nodes.name: MediaPlayer.svelte
nodes.summary: First-class AV media player for sound and video objects with a transcript spine, synchronized playback (click to seek), temporal marker strip, and spatiotemporal video box overlays.

nodes.name: NarrativeReader.svelte
nodes.summary: Prose-led narrative layout reading experience with a section spine driving the canvas, marker notes with popups, reading legend support, and deep-link arrival handling.

nodes.name: NoteLightbox.svelte
nodes.summary: Modal lightbox for note media with a carousel of images/video/audio and the note text displayed alongside, navigable via arrow keys and dismissible via Escape or backdrop click.

nodes.name: NoteMedia.svelte
nodes.summary: Note media tile component rendering image thumbnails, video posters, and audio waveform placeholders as clickable tiles that open the lightbox, with broken-media fallback handling.

nodes.name: ObjectGrid.svelte
nodes.summary: Exhibit object overview grid displaying IIIF thumbnails on the dark light table, each card showing its note count and opening the reader on selection.

nodes.name: Reader.svelte
nodes.summary: Object reader with a read-only OSD canvas, prev/next carousel for multi-object exhibits, annotation list with detail state and popup, reading legend, and note media lightbox integration.

nodes.name: ReadingLegend.svelte
nodes.summary: Canvas overlay radio-group legend for selecting among competing readings (cipher/hoax/abjad), rendering colour-coded options with a description of the active reading.

nodes.name: ViewerShell.svelte
nodes.summary: Single client-routed shell managing viewer mode detection (hosted vs portable), hash-based routing, gallery loading with single-exhibit collapse, and transitions between empty hall, gallery, and exhibit views.

nodes.name: markers.css
nodes.summary: Stroke-over-stroke marker styling for Annotorious annotation layers, providing contrast-adaptive markers with a warm-paper light halo and dark drop-shadow that work on any background.

nodes.name: voynich-reading.astro

nodes.name: canvasIdFor

nodes.name: openPortableLibrary

nodes.name: isPortable

nodes.name: modeFromProbe
nodes.summary: Pure classifier determining viewer mode from a probe result, treating only HTTP 404 as the portable signal and all other failures as errors.

nodes.name: probeViewerMode

nodes.name: openLibraryFromSrc

nodes.name: loadPublishedExhibit

nodes.name: getLog
nodes.summary: Looks up the annotation log for a given exhibit ID from the pre-built logs map.

nodes.name: heads.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests for the heads projection function that computes the current live state from an append-only log.

nodes.name: heads.ts
nodes.summary: Heads projection (ADR-0003 / Q-3): computes the current live annotation records from an append-only log, excluding tombstoned entries and supporting plural heads for unresolved merges.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel file re-exporting all public modules of the annotation spine (log, merge, heads, serialize, deserialize, persist).
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript barrel file using re-exports from six spine modules.

nodes.name: interop.test.ts
nodes.summary: Interop tests verifying compatibility between the append-only log format and WADM-compliant consumers.

nodes.name: merge.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests for the version-DAG merge engine: lineage tracking, common ancestor detection, merge classification, and conflict resolution.

nodes.name: merge.ts
nodes.summary: Version-DAG merge engine (ADR-0003 / Q-3) for the annotation log: lineage, ancestry, common ancestor, heads, merge classification, and conflict resolution.

nodes.name: persist.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests for serializing and deserializing annotation logs to and from the OPFS-based annotation directory structure.

nodes.name: persist.ts
nodes.summary: Persistence layer for annotation logs: writes annotation heads and history pages to an OPFS directory and reads them back.

nodes.name: resolve.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests for conflict resolution in the annotation log, verifying manual tiebreaking and resolution record generation.

nodes.name: projectHeads
nodes.summary: Projects an append-only annotation log to its currently-live head records, excluding tombstoned entries and supporting plural heads for unresolved merges.

nodes.name: lineage
nodes.summary: Traces the revision ancestry chain from a given revision back to root.

nodes.name: ancestors
nodes.summary: Computes the full set of ancestor revisions for a given revision using DFS traversal.

nodes.name: commonAncestor
nodes.summary: Finds the most recent common ancestor revision between two revisions.

nodes.name: headsOf
nodes.summary: Returns all head revisions for a logical ID (plural for unresolved merges).

nodes.name: mergeLogs
nodes.summary: Merges two annotation logs (local and incoming) into a single log without duplicates.

nodes.name: classifyMerge
nodes.summary: Classifies the merge relationship between two revisions (ancestor, descendant, or diverged).

nodes.name: classifyLogical
nodes.summary: Classifies the merge relationship between two logs for a specific logical ID.

nodes.name: conflictTiebreak
nodes.summary: Tiebreaker comparator for conflicting revisions choosing the lexicographically highest rev.

nodes.name: resolveConflict
nodes.summary: Creates a merge-commit resolution record that reconciles diverged heads for a logical ID.

nodes.name: writeAnnotations
nodes.summary: Serializes annotation log to an OPFS directory as heads pages and history pages.

nodes.name: readAnnotations
nodes.summary: Reads annotation heads and history pages from an OPFS directory back into a log.

nodes.name: main.ts
nodes.summary: Studio application entry point that mounts the Svelte App component into the DOM and imports global CSS (tokens, Annotorious, markers).
nodes.languageNotes: Phase-0 boundary shell — Phase 2 mounts the adopted editor shell here.

nodes.name: App.svelte
nodes.summary: Main Studio editor shell orchestrating the full exhibit authoring workflow: library management, annotation session lifecycle, image/AV object editing, narrative spine authoring, rights/credit editing, publish flow, and folder/zip persistence with three-config binding.
nodes.languageNotes: Svelte 5 runes ($state/$derived/$effect) throughout with reactive store pattern for exhibit/library metadata.

nodes.name: AvEditor.svelte
nodes.summary: Audio/video annotation surface — WaveSurfer waveform for audio with drag-to-cue regions, and a <video> overlay with percent-based frame-draw boxes combined with temporal selectors for spatiotemporal annotations.
nodes.languageNotes: Dynamic import of WaveSurfer + Regions plugin; percent-based spatial coordinates for resolution-independent video frame markup.

nodes.name: bake.ts
nodes.summary: Display-master image baking: EXIF orientation-aware upright PNG generation and dimension-capped downscaling for phone photo imports, using browser Canvas API with createImageBitmap.
nodes.languageNotes: Browser-only Canvas API — createImageBitmap with imageOrientation:from-image decodes already-upright; verified in browser, not headless.

nodes.name: binding.ts
nodes.summary: Browser capability detection and persistence glue for the three-config library binding system: folder picker (Chromium), file stream save, zip download, recents I/O, and active binding serialization to localStorage.

nodes.name: CmdK.svelte
nodes.summary: Intra-library linking palette (Cmd+K) for citing notes and exhibits within the library, presenting a searchable catalog card list with encoded archie: URI insertion at the cursor.

nodes.name: DetailsEditor.svelte
nodes.summary: Shared per-level details form rendered at library, exhibit, and object scopes: title, description, and delegated RightsEditor for credit/license fields.

nodes.name: ExhibitOverview.svelte
nodes.summary: Exhibit overview-as-canvas: zoomable/pannable plate layout of objects in reading order, with drag-reorder, layout picker, and header drawer for exhibit-level rights/identity editing.

nodes.name: handles-db.ts
nodes.summary: IndexedDB persistence layer for FileSystemHandles, enabling folder-bound library projects to survive page reloads via structured-clone handle storage with permission re-acquisition.
nodes.languageNotes: Standalone IndexedDB wrapper with no external dependency — uses raw IDBRequest promise-wrapping pattern.

nodes.name: IdentityPrompt.svelte
nodes.summary: Collaborator identity prompt displayed at first merge interaction, asking for a display name used as lastEditor in the merge DAG — shown at the moment it acquires meaning, not at launch.

nodes.name: LayoutPicker.svelte
nodes.summary: Layout selection component for choosing an exhibit's reading intent (single/grid/narrative), shaping the published IIIF presentation.

nodes.name: LibraryHome.svelte
nodes.summary: Library home screen — curator's table of exhibit plates with the project bar (binding status, save/open/close), recents list, exhibit grid with template badges, and new-exhibit creation tile.

nodes.name: markers.css
nodes.summary: Stroke-over-stroke Annotorious marker styling providing contrast against any background using a warm-paper halo paired with a dark drop-shadow, without pixel-sampling the underlying image.

nodes.name: MergeReview.svelte
nodes.summary: Merge conflict review interface for collaborative annotation sessions, displaying conflicting edits with the merge DAG's lastEditor stamps.

nodes.name: NarrativeEditor.svelte
nodes.summary: Narrative spine editor for exhibit sections: each beat targets an object with a camera frame (xywh box or AV t= fragment), prose with intra-library linking, and cross-exhibit note references.

nodes.name: PropsDrawer.svelte
nodes.summary: Slide-in right drawer panel with scrim for transient property editing (rights, metadata) at library and exhibit levels, paper-toned surface hosting the shared editors.

nodes.name: Publish.svelte
nodes.summary: GitHub Pages publish dialog with PAT entry (never persisted), include-originals opt-in, broken-link surfacing, and publish progress reporting through the publishToGitHub pipeline.

nodes.name: PublishDialog.svelte
nodes.summary: Unified publish dialog with destination choice (local folder/zip or GitHub Pages), phase-state machine for progress feedback, and guarded execution.

nodes.name: RightsEditor.svelte
nodes.summary: Shared credit/license rights form rendered at library/exhibit/object levels, using curator-facing copy with license dropdown from the Core license registry and attribution field.

nodes.name: shortcuts.ts
nodes.summary: Single-source keyboard shortcut registry driving both the help cheat-sheet and handler matching: defines all shortcuts with curator-voice labels, grouped by context (Anywhere/Image/Audio).

nodes.name: ShortcutsHelp.svelte
nodes.summary: Keyboard shortcuts cheat-sheet overlay generated from the shortcut registry, ensuring the help display never drifts from the wired handlers — paper card presentation over dark scrim.

nodes.name: store.ts
nodes.summary: OPFS-based working store for the Studio: manages exhibit/library metadata persistence, imported asset file storage with EXIF orientation transform, lazy blob URL resolution for media playback, and annotation directory lifecycle.

nodes.name: bakeDisplayMaster
nodes.summary: Re-encodes an uploaded image to an upright PNG display master via the browser Canvas API, optionally capped to a maximum dimension while preserving aspect ratio.

nodes.name: downscaleIfNeeded
nodes.summary: Decodes an image once to check dimensions and downscales only if it exceeds the cap, preserving the source format (JPEG stays JPEG) to avoid unnecessary PNG re-encoding bloat.

nodes.name: supportsFolderPicker
nodes.summary: Detects whether the browser supports the File System Access API's showDirectoryPicker (Chromium only).

nodes.name: pickFolder
nodes.summary: Prompts the user to select a directory for folder-bound library binding, returning a FileSystemDirectoryHandle or null if cancelled.

nodes.name: downloadZip
nodes.summary: Triggers a browser download of a Uint8Array as a .archie.zip file via a temporary anchor element with deferred object URL revocation.

nodes.name: supportsFileStreamSave
nodes.summary: Detects whether the browser supports streaming file saves via showSaveFilePicker (Chromium).

nodes.name: saveZipToDisk
nodes.summary: Saves a ZipFilesystem to disk: streams chunk-by-chunk via showSaveFilePicker on Chromium, falls back to eager in-memory zip + anchor download elsewhere.

nodes.name: zipNameFor
nodes.summary: Derives a filesystem-safe .archie.zip filename from a project title by lowercasing and replacing non-alphanumeric characters.

nodes.name: loadRecents
nodes.summary: Loads the recent-projects list from localStorage with tolerant fallback to empty array on corruption or unavailability.

nodes.name: saveRecents
nodes.summary: Persists the recent-projects list to localStorage, silently no-oping if storage is unavailable (private mode).

nodes.name: loadLastBinding
nodes.summary: Restores the active binding descriptor from localStorage so the UI shows continuity across reloads; returns unbound if nothing stored.

nodes.name: saveLastBinding
nodes.summary: Persists or clears the active binding descriptor in localStorage.

nodes.name: putHandle
nodes.summary: Stores a FileSystemHandle in IndexedDB under the given key for cross-session folder binding persistence.

nodes.name: getHandle
nodes.summary: Retrieves a previously-stored FileSystemDirectoryHandle from IndexedDB by key, or null if absent.

nodes.name: deleteHandle
nodes.summary: Removes a stored FileSystemHandle from IndexedDB (e.g. on project close or lost binding removal).

nodes.name: queryPermission
nodes.summary: Queries the current permission state for a stored FileSystemHandle without prompting the user.

nodes.name: requestPermission
nodes.summary: Re-requests permission for a stored FileSystemHandle — must be called from a user gesture.

nodes.name: matches
nodes.summary: Single keydown-to-shortcut matcher for every entry in the keyboard registry, ensuring handlers and the cheat-sheet speak the same vocabulary.

nodes.name: typingInField
nodes.summary: Returns true when focus is in a text field so bare-letter shortcuts do not fire while the user is typing.

nodes.name: openExhibitAnnotationsDir
nodes.summary: Opens or creates the OPFS annotation directory for a given exhibit slug, using legacy path for the sample exhibit and per-slug paths for others.

nodes.name: loadLibraryMeta
nodes.summary: Reads the authored library structure from OPFS library.json, returning null on first run or when OPFS is unavailable.

nodes.name: saveLibraryMeta
nodes.summary: Persists the authored library structure to OPFS library.json, no-oping if OPFS is unavailable.

nodes.name: saveAssetFile
nodes.summary: Stores an imported display-master image or AV file into the exhibit's OPFS assets directory.

nodes.name: saveOriginalFile
nodes.summary: Preserves the untouched original image file alongside the display master in assets-original/ for citation provenance.

nodes.name: assetSize
nodes.summary: Returns the byte size of a stored asset using File.size (metadata only, no byte read) for the pre-zip memory ceiling estimate.

nodes.name: readAssetUrl
nodes.summary: Resolves a stored asset to a fresh blob: URL with MIME type restoration from extension for AV playback compatibility.

nodes.name: clearExhibitAnnotations
nodes.summary: Removes an exhibit's OPFS annotation directory so it reseeds from code defaults on next open, used when bundled exhibit definitions change.

nodes.name: readAssetBlob
nodes.summary: Returns a stored asset as a lazy OPFS File (Blob) without reading into the JS heap, enabling streaming zip writes for the publish path.

nodes.name: readOriginalBytes
nodes.summary: Reads a preserved original file's bytes from assets-original/ for opt-in citation publish.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with docs/README.md to understand what Archie is: a platform for turning media into interactive, linkable exhibits. Annotate deep-zoom images, audio, and video in the browser, then publish a self-contained static site. This document establishes the project's scope and terminology before you encounter any code.

tour.title: Architecture Context
tour.description: CONTEXT.md is the comprehensive design and architecture document for Archie. It defines the project's terminology, design philosophy, and key architectural decisions. Reading this gives you the mental model--exhibits, annotations, the spine log, IIIF projection, and the two-app architecture (Studio + Viewer)--that the rest of the tour builds on.

tour.title: Monorepo Organization
tour.description: Archie is a pnpm workspace monorepo. The root package.json declares five workspace members: the pure-TS @render/core engine, the @render/svelte and @render/mount rendering adapters, the Studio Svelte authoring SPA, and the Viewer Astro static site. The pnpm-workspace.yaml file defines the packages/ directories, while the root tsconfig.base.json enforces strict TypeScript across all packages. Understanding this layout explains where each piece of functionality lives.

tour.title: Core Data Model: W3C Annotations
tour.description: The entire Archie data model is built on the W3C Web Annotation Data Model (WADM). The types.ts file defines the full annotation model with bodies, targets, selectors, and the Archie-specific spine metadata constants. These TypeScript interfaces are the vocabulary used by every other module in the system--from the Studio's store to the Viewer's rendering pipeline.
tour.languageLesson: TypeScript discriminated unions and branded types enforce domain constraints at the type level. The AnnotationBody union with discriminators like TextBody, ImageBody, and AudioBody ensures that only valid body shapes reach the runtime. This pattern is used throughout the codebase to model the W3C annotation spec without runtime validation overhead.

tour.title: Annotation Spine: Append-Only Log
tour.description: The spine is Archie's core innovation (ADR-0003): an append-only annotation log where every create, edit, and delete operation produces a new entry. The log.ts module provides appendNew, appendEdit, and appendDelete operations that produce properly sequenced entries. The session.ts module wraps the log in a transactional CRUD API with automatic conflict detection, ensuring that concurrent edits produce a valid DAG rather than losing data.
tour.languageLesson: An append-only log for mutable data is counterintuitive: every 'edit' is actually a new annotation that supersedes the previous version. The spine resolves the current state by projecting the log forward through its heads. This avoids the lost-update problem entirely--conflicts produce forks in the DAG rather than silent overwrites--at the cost of needing a projection step to compute the latest version.

tour.title: IIIF Publication Format
tour.description: Exhibits are published as IIIF Presentation 3 manifests. The manifest.ts module projects an Exhibit object into IIIF Canvases with painting Annotations, supporting image, audio, and video media. The resolve.ts module classifies image sources (direct URLs vs. IIIF Image API services) and generates the appropriate tile-source descriptors. Together they bridge Archie's internal model to the interoperable IIIF standard.

tour.title: Studio Authoring SPA
tour.description: The Studio is a Svelte 5 single-page application for creating and editing exhibits. Its HTML entry point at index.html loads the fonts and mounts the Svelte app shell. App.svelte orchestrates the full authoring workflow: library management, annotation session lifecycle, image and AV object editing, narrative spine authoring, rights editing, and the publish flow. The store.ts module provides the OPFS-based working store that manages exhibit metadata persistence, imported asset files, and annotation directory lifecycles.
tour.languageLesson: Svelte 5 uses runes ($state, $derived, $effect) for reactivity instead of the older store API. The Studio's store.ts is named for its role (application state) but uses Svelte 5's $state rune internally. Components declare reactive state at the module top level, and Svelte's compiler wires the dependency graph automatically--no manual subscriptions or selectors needed.

tour.title: Studio Persistence Layer
tour.description: The binding.ts module implements the three-config library binding system: folder-based (OPFS), single-file zip, and auto-detection between them. It handles browser capability detection, persistence glue, and switching between storage backends. This is the layer that makes Archie work as a purely client-side application--no server required for authoring.

tour.title: Viewer Static Site
tour.description: The Viewer is an Astro-built static site for published exhibits. index.astro is the main entry page that bootstraps the Svelte ViewerShell island with OpenSeadragon-dependent browser-only rendering. The published.ts module provides a unified read API that abstracts over hosted and portable (zipped) library access, giving the viewer components a single interface for loading exhibits regardless of deployment mode.
tour.languageLesson: Astro's islands architecture serves static HTML by default and hydrates interactive 'islands' on the client. The ViewerShell island is marked client:visible so it only loads JavaScript when the component scrolls into view. This keeps the initial page load lightweight while still allowing full Svelte interactivity for the annotation viewer.

tour.title: Viewer Exhibit UI
tour.description: ViewerShell.svelte is the single client-routed shell that manages viewer mode detection (hosted vs portable), hash-based routing, and layout loading. ExhibitView.svelte is the layout orchestrator that loads published exhibit data, resolves the layout type (single image, grid, or narrative), and renders the appropriate viewing experience. The route.ts module provides the hash-based router that parses location hashes into structured ViewerRoute objects for deep-linkable gallery and exhibit navigation.

tour.title: Rendering Pipeline
tour.description: The rendering chain runs from Svelte components down to the browser's DOM. Canvas.svelte is the Svelte 5 adapter component for deep-zoom image rendering with annotation overlays. controller.ts bridges the imperative OpenSeadragon/Annotorious wiring (ADR-0002) into Svelte's reactive world using $state and $effect. mount.ts at the @render/mount level provides the framework-free MountSurface factory that instantiates OpenSeadragon and wires the Annotorious annotation overlay--the primitive that both Svelte and any future framework adapter depend on.
tour.languageLesson: OpenSeadragon provides the deep-zoom tile rendering engine. Annotorious overlays W3C Web Annotations directly onto the canvas. The controller pattern (ADR-0002) wraps this imperative pairing in a reactive binding: changes to annotation state flow into OSD via the controller, and user interactions (drawing shapes, selecting regions) flow back out as annotation events. Svelte's $effect bridges the gap cleanly.

tour.title: Publishing Engine
tour.description: The publishing pipeline transforms a Library plus its annotation log into a deployable static site. site.ts serializes the IIIF Collection and Manifests to disk, writes the per-exhibit annotation pages, and assembles the complete directory structure. portable.ts implements the portable read seam (ADR-0010): opening a published .archie.zip and resolving /assets/ media to blob: URLs so the viewer works entirely from a single zip file without a network request.
tour.languageLesson: The zip filesystem abstraction uses streaming writes to build .archie.zip files without loading all assets into memory. The portable seam reverses this: it reads the zip, extracts asset bytes into blob: URLs via URL.createObjectURL, and presents a unified filesystem interface to the viewer. This enables fully offline exhibit distribution--a single .archie.zip contains the viewer, assets, annotations, and IIIF manifests.

tour.title: Build and Deployment
tour.description: The deploy.yml GitHub Actions workflow is triggered on push to main, building the Svelte Studio and Viewer application with Node 24. The build-gh-pages.sh script assembles the deploy directory: it builds the Studio SPA, builds the Viewer Astro site, copies in published exhibit data, and writes a landing page. The result is a fully self-contained static site deployable to GitHub Pages--no server, no database, no build step needed after publish.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions workflows define CI/CD as YAML with three layers: on (triggers), jobs (parallel units), and steps (sequential commands). The Archie workflow uses a single build job with Node 24 setup, dependency caching via pnpm, and a deploy step that runs the shell script. Matrix strategies would allow testing across multiple Node versions or OS targets when needed.
